reasoning: Pixel art is largely clean and grid-aligned at 8x zoom. Palette is limited (pinks, oranges, dark purples for base) with hard tone transitions — no gradient blending detected. Background is solid dark with no noise pixels. Eye/center detail is coherent and clean. Main concerns: (1) Diamond body shows subtle left-right asymmetry in pixel placement on the lower-left and lower-right facets — a few pixels break expected mirror symmetry. (2) Pedestal/base area has color variation that is slightly inconsistent: some gray-purple tiles appear without clear shading logic, suggesting possible AI fill rather than deliberate dithering. (3) Two to three stray dark pixels appear along the lower edge of the body where it meets the base, without contributing to a clean outline. Outline weight is mostly 1px and consistent around the diamond, but drops to 0px at a few corners of the pedestal. No melting, no color bleed, no AA blurring, no semantic failures — design reads clearly as an orb-boss on a pedestal. Scores marginal rather than fail because issues are minor and the overall pixel discipline is high.
